---
title: "yuzhou-mac-theme: Terminal Setup Guide"
author: "Yuzhou Chang"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-copy: true
    theme: cosmo
    highlight-style: github
  pdf:
    toc: true
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: false
---

# Overview

This guide walks through setting up a clean, fast, cross-platform terminal
on **macOS** and **Linux** using:

- **[Ghostty](https://ghostty.org)** — modern GPU-accelerated terminal
- **[zsh](https://www.zsh.org/)** with autosuggestions + syntax highlighting
- **[Starship](https://starship.rs/)** — fast cross-shell prompt
- **[eza](https://eza.rocks/)** — `ls` replacement with colors and icons
- **JetBrainsMono Nerd Font** — monospaced font with developer icons

::: {.callout-note}
All code, configs, and the one-shot installer live at
[github.com/BMEngineeR/yuzhou-mac-theme](https://github.com/BMEngineeR/yuzhou-mac-theme).
:::

# Before & After

::: {#fig-before-after layout-ncol=2}
![Before — plain bash/zsh, no icons, no colors](images/01-before.png){#fig-before width=100% fig-alt="Plain terminal with default prompt and uncolored ls output"}

![After — Starship prompt, colored `eza` listing, icons](images/02-after.png){#fig-after width=100% fig-alt="Themed terminal with Starship prompt, colored file listing with icons"}

The same shell, before and after this setup.
:::

# Prerequisites

| Component | macOS                                | Linux                              |
|-----------|--------------------------------------|------------------------------------|
| OS        | macOS 13+                            | Ubuntu 22.04+ / Fedora 38+ / Arch  |
| Terminal  | Ghostty 1.0+ ([install](https://ghostty.org/download)) | Ghostty 1.0+ (same)                |
| Shell     | zsh 5.9+ (default on modern macOS)   | zsh 5.9+ (`apt/dnf/pacman install zsh`) |
| Git       | preinstalled                         | preinstalled or via package manager |

# One-shot install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/BMEngineeR/yuzhou-mac-theme.git ~/yuzhou-mac-theme
cd ~/yuzhou-mac-theme
bash install.sh
```

The installer is idempotent — re-running it is safe. Existing
`~/.zshrc` and `~/.config/ghostty/config` are backed up with a timestamp
before being replaced by symlinks into the repo.

## What the installer does

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A[bash install.sh] --> B{uname -s}
    B -->|Darwin| C[Homebrew]
    B -->|Linux| D{/etc/os-release}
    D -->|debian/ubuntu| E[apt]
    D -->|fedora/rhel| F[dnf]
    D -->|arch| G[pacman]
    C --> H[Install: starship, eza, zsh-autosuggestions,<br/>zsh-syntax-highlighting, gh + Nerd Font cask]
    E --> I[Install equivalent packages]
    F --> I
    G --> I
    I --> J[Download Nerd Font zip<br/>fc-cache -f]
    H --> K[Symlink configs into $HOME]
    J --> K
    K --> L[Done — exec zsh]
```

::: {#fig-installer-output}
![Installer run on a clean Mac](images/03-installer-run.png){width=100% fig-alt="Terminal output showing install.sh progress with brew installs and link confirmations"}

Sample output from running `bash install.sh` on a fresh Mac.
:::

# What you get

## Starship prompt

The Starship prompt automatically shows:

- Current directory (with shortened paths)
- Git branch and status
- Active language version (when in a project: Node, Python, Rust, Go, …)
- Command execution time when commands take longer than a threshold
- Non-zero exit status from the previous command

::: {#fig-starship}
![Starship prompt inside a git repo](images/04-starship-git.png){width=80% fig-alt="Prompt showing folder name and git branch with modified file indicator"}
:::

Example interactive session (simulated output):

```bash
~ on  main
❯ cd Documents/GitHub/yuzhou-mac-theme

yuzhou-mac-theme on  main [!?]
❯ git status --short
 M README.md
?? docs/

yuzhou-mac-theme on  main [!?] took 3ms
❯
```

## Colored `ls` with `eza`

The aliases below replace `ls`:

```bash
alias ls='eza --icons --group-directories-first'
alias ll='eza -lh --icons --group-directories-first --git'
alias la='eza -lah --icons --group-directories-first --git'
alias lt='eza --tree --level=2 --icons --group-directories-first'
```

Color coding by file type:

| Type              | Color   | Examples                          |
|-------------------|---------|-----------------------------------|
| Directory         | Blue    | `src/`, `node_modules/`           |
| Executable script | Green   | `install.sh`, `*.py` with `+x`    |
| Symlink           | Cyan    | `current -> v1.2.3/`              |
| Archive           | Red     | `*.zip`, `*.tar.gz`               |
| Image             | Magenta | `*.png`, `*.jpg`                  |
| Plain file        | White   | `README.md`, `*.txt`              |

::: {#fig-eza}
![`ll` output inside the repo](images/05-eza-ll.png){width=90% fig-alt="Long-format directory listing with icons, colored file types, and git status column"}

`ll` inside the `yuzhou-mac-theme` repo, showing icons, colored types,
and the `--git` status column.
:::

## Syntax highlighting & autosuggestions

As you type, `zsh-syntax-highlighting` colors valid commands green and
invalid ones red, while `zsh-autosuggestions` shows a grayed-out
suggestion from your history that you accept by pressing **→**.

::: {#fig-zsh-plugins}
![Live highlighting and suggestions while typing](images/06-zsh-plugins.png){width=90% fig-alt="Terminal showing a green-highlighted valid command and a gray autosuggestion to the right"}
:::

# Customization

## Switching the color theme

List built-in themes:

```bash
ghostty +list-themes
```

Edit `~/.config/ghostty/config`:

```toml
theme = "Catppuccin Mocha"
```

Popular options:

::: {layout-ncol=3}
- `Catppuccin Mocha`
- `Tokyo Night`
- `Dracula`
- `Nord`
- `Solarized Dark`
- `GruvboxDark` (default)
:::

::: {#fig-themes layout-ncol=2}
![Catppuccin Mocha](images/07-theme-catppuccin.png){#fig-cat width=100%}

![Tokyo Night](images/08-theme-tokyo.png){#fig-tokyo width=100%}

Two popular alternative themes.
:::

## Customizing Starship

Generate a starter config:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.config
starship preset nerd-font-symbols -o ~/.config/starship.toml
```

Then edit `~/.config/starship.toml`. The Starship docs list every
module: <https://starship.rs/config/>.

## Customizing `eza` colors

Set `EZA_COLORS` in `~/.zshrc` to override defaults. Example —
make directories bold magenta:

```bash
export EZA_COLORS="di=1;35"
```

Full syntax: <https://github.com/eza-community/eza/blob/main/man/eza_colors.5.md>.

# Verification

After running the installer and `exec zsh`, confirm everything is wired up:

```bash
# Prompt — Starship should be active
echo $STARSHIP_SHELL          # "zsh"

# eza — should print a version
eza --version | head -1

# Plugins — these arrays are populated when the plugins are loaded
typeset -p _ZSH_AUTOSUGGEST_OPTIONS 2>&1 | head -1
typeset -p ZSH_HIGHLIGHT_VERSION

# Font — Ghostty needs Nerd Font for icons to render
fc-list 2>/dev/null | grep -i "JetBrainsMono Nerd" | head -1
```

::: {#fig-verify}
![Output of the verification commands](images/09-verify.png){width=90% fig-alt="Terminal showing the verification commands all returning expected values"}
:::

# Troubleshooting

::: {.callout-warning}
## Icons render as squares or "?"

The terminal is not using a Nerd Font. Fix:

1. Confirm the font is installed: `fc-list | grep -i nerd` (Linux) or
   `ls ~/Library/Fonts | grep -i nerd` (macOS).
2. Edit `~/.config/ghostty/config` and set
   `font-family = "JetBrainsMono Nerd Font"`.
3. Restart Ghostty (the config does not hot-reload font changes
   reliably).
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## `command not found: starship`

Either Starship didn't install, or your `PATH` doesn't include its
install location. Check:

```bash
# macOS / Linuxbrew
ls $(brew --prefix)/bin/starship

# Linux native install (from starship.rs installer)
ls ~/.local/bin/starship /usr/local/bin/starship
```

Re-run the installer; it is idempotent.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Plugins not loading

Run `zsh -xv 2>&1 | grep -i 'no such file' | head` to see which path
the `source` call tried. Then check the appropriate location for your
package manager (see the comments in `zshrc`).
:::

# Rendering this document

Install Quarto:

```bash
# macOS
brew install --cask quarto

# Linux
# Download .deb / .rpm from https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/
```

Render:

```bash
cd docs/
quarto render setup.qmd            # HTML output → setup.html
quarto render setup.qmd --to pdf   # PDF output  → setup.pdf
```

# Appendix: file layout

```
yuzhou-mac-theme/
├── README.md
├── install.sh          # macOS + Linux installer
├── zshrc               # symlinked to ~/.zshrc
├── ghostty/
│   └── config          # symlinked to ~/.config/ghostty/config
└── docs/
    ├── setup.qmd       # this document
    └── images/         # screenshots referenced above
```
